access: authenticated browser UI (institutional: University of the Cumberlands)
method: Advanced-search form submit was blocked by the SPA handler; used the reproducible results.uri GET endpoint instead (session-scoped). Probe lookups run one-at-a-time with delays; no bulk export, no CAPTCHA bypass.

registration_query.query: TITLE-ABS-KEY(("large language model" OR LLM) AND (agent OR autonomous) AND (benchmark OR evaluation)) AND PUBYEAR > 2022 AND PUBYEAR < 2027

probe_method: TITLE-scoped lookups (TITLE("<name>")); found=1 iff the canonical benchmark paper's own title is the indexed record. This is stricter and more accurate than the ALL-field probes used as a first pass (ALL() matched reference lists and returned hundreds of citing works).

probe_lookups.matched_title: SWE-BENCH: CAN LANGUAGE MODELS RESOLVE REAL-WORLD GITHUB ISSUES?
GAIA: A BENCHMARK FOR GENERAL AI ASSISTANTS
AppWorld: A Controllable World of Apps and People for Benchmarking Interactive Coding Agents
OSWORLD: Benchmarking Multimodal Agents for Open-Ended Tasks in Real Computer Environments
MLE-BENCH: EVALUATING MACHINE LEARNING AGENTS ON MACHINE LEARNING ENGINEERING

probe_lookups.note: 0 results; Feb-2025 arXiv preprint not yet indexed in Scopus

probe_lookups.matched_title: AgentBoard: An Analytical Evaluation Board of Multi-turn LLM Agents
VisualWebArena: Evaluating Multimodal Agents on Realistic Visually Grounded Web Tasks
IDENTIFYING THE RISKS OF LM AGENTS WITH AN LM-EMULATED SANDBOX

interpretation.key_finding: Unlike IEEE/ACM/ScienceDirect (each 0/10, hosting only citing works), Scopus indexes the canonical benchmark PAPER ITSELF for 9 of 10 probes (all but DataSciBench, a 2025 preprint). Scopus covers the ICLR/NeurIPS/ACL/AAAI proceedings where these benchmarks are archivally published. Every one of those 9 is also present in OpenAlex (Scopus_only = 0), so the probe gap against the full source union remains 0.0.
interpretation.implication_for_recall: Scopus is the first genuinely independent index that corroborates OpenAlex on canonical coverage. Its 9/10 recall and 9/9 overlap with OpenAlex directly support the probe-proxy conclusion (canonical recall ~complete) and confirm that the unstable Crossref capture-recapture point value (implied 7.7%) is a heterogeneity artifact, not a real recall measurement.
interpretation.corpus_capture_recapture: Not computed at corpus level: the 4,836 figure is the unscreened TITLE-ABS-KEY field count, not a same-population screened include set comparable to the 259 OpenAlex includes, and record-level overlap m would require export (gated). Reporting the probe-set 2x2 and registered count only; capture-recapture corpus N_hat from Scopus = UNRESOLVED:export_gated.
interpretation.label: estimate-supporting evidence; probe columns verified via live authenticated UI
